name: Travis AFB
profile: Military-friendly Travis AFB housing snapshot anchored to Fairfield / Vacaville / Dixon, built for PCSUnited base-level market overviews.

market_metrics.market_type_summary: High demand with high entry costs

mortgage_assumptions.defaults_note: Based on March 2026 California VA loan rates.

notes: Property taxes and insurance materially affect true monthly ownership cost near Travis.
Insurance and HOA should be included in all-in housing cost comparisons.

avg_home_mortgage_monthly.notes: VA loan estimate; high entry costs require significant BAH alignment.

crime_status.metro_shreveport_bossier: Fairfield has higher crime rates in specific southern corridors; Vacaville is generally preferred for safety.

school_quality.on_base: Travis Unified School District (TUSD) serves on-base residents.
school_quality.off_base: Vacaville Unified and TUSD schools in Fairfield/Vacaville are highly rated.

rental_vacancy.notes: Tight rental market; demand is high due to proximity to the Bay Area.

rental_metrics.notes: Tight rental market; demand is high due to proximity to the Bay Area.

military_lifestyle_fit.description: Highly integrated with local TUSD; military presence is a major economic driver for Solano County.

financial_brief.affordability_summary: High cost of living requires careful budgeting even with high California BAH rates.
financial_brief.buyer_opportunity: Strongest in the newer Vacaville developments and Dixon.

market_bluf.bluf_summary: Stable value but high entry costs relative to other AFBs.

summary_points: Travis Air Force Base sits in the Fairfield / Vacaville / Dixon housing market with base-specific affordability, commute, and neighborhood tradeoffs.
Current market trend is approximately 0.8% year-over-year, which should shape buyer leverage and pricing discipline.
High cost of living requires careful budgeting even with high California BAH rates.
Neighborhood quality varies across Fairfield, Vacaville, Dixon and other nearby corridors — citywide averages should not be the only filter.
Stable value but high entry costs relative to other AFBs.
opportunities: Travis military demand can support durable housing relevance for disciplined buyers and landlords.
Local inventory and pricing may offer opportunity for households that stress-test taxes, insurance, and commute together.
Fairfield and nearby corridors may offer stronger family or value positioning depending on assignment length.
Assignment-length clarity can improve rent-versus-buy decisions near Travis.
risks: Neighborhood quality, schools, commute, and monthly carrying costs vary across the Fairfield / Vacaville / Dixon market.
Property taxes, insurance, HOA, and utilities can widen the gap between list price and true monthly payment.
Market averages can hide weak submarkets — property-level review matters.
Some BAH profiles may be better served by renting than buying in this market.
buyer_notes: Travis buyers should compare all-in monthly cost, not just principal and interest.
Verify school zones, commute timing, and BAH fit by exact address before signing.
Strongest in the newer Vacaville developments and Dixon.
This market rewards disciplined buyers who test the commute during expected report hours.
seller_notes: Sellers should price against current inventory and days on market rather than older peak assumptions.
Move-in-ready presentation and realistic pricing matter when buyers have leverage.
Military-adjacent commute convenience can be a listing strength if framed clearly.
investor_angles: Military turnover near Travis can support rental demand, but vacancy and maintenance should be modeled conservatively.
Investors should underwrite taxes, insurance, HOA, and property condition — not just acquisition price.
Practical commute corridors often outperform generic metro averages for military renter demand.
buyer_guidance: Use all-in monthly payment as the primary screen.
Compare taxes, insurance, commute, school quality, and resale flexibility before locking a neighborhood.
Do not choose housing based only on distance to Travis.
A slightly lower purchase price can materially improve monthly PCS flexibility.
seller_guidance: Price against active inventory and days on market.
Condition and presentation matter when buyers can compare more options.
Military commute convenience can help positioning if described accurately.
landlord_notes: Military renter demand can improve occupancy durability near Travis.
Model vacancy, repairs, insurance, and turnover conservatively.
Stable spread matters more than headline affordability alone.

market.market_type_summary: High demand with high entry costs

base_profile.host_or_major_command: 60th Air Mobility Wing / Air Mobility Command
base_profile.primary_mission_summary: Travis AFB supports air mobility and airlift missions in Solano County between the Bay Area and Sacramento with high California housing costs.
base_profile.pcs_personality: Highly integrated with local TUSD; military presence is a major economic driver for Solano County.
base_profile.base_bluf: Stable value but high entry costs relative to other AFBs.

user_positioning.military_family_bluf: Prioritize school-zone verification, BAH fit, commute testing, childcare access, and total monthly housing cost before signing near Travis.
user_positioning.single_airman_bluf: Prioritize commute simplicity, affordability, rental flexibility, and access to daily services around Fairfield.
user_positioning.realtor_bluf: Lead with BAH realism, commute risk, school-zone confidence, and all-in payment math for Travis clients — not generic Fairfield / Vacaville / Dixon stats alone.
user_positioning.investor_bluf: Rental demand near Travis is influenced by PCS turnover, training/permanent-party mix, and practical commute corridors.

commute_intelligence.commute_bluf: For Travis, gate access and traffic timing often matter more than straight-line distance.
primary_commute_factors: Shift schedule or early report time

commute_bands.label: Close-in
commute_bands.fit: Best for users prioritizing proximity and daily convenience
commute_bands.watchout: Close-in areas still need property, school, and safety screening.

commute_bands.label: Balanced
commute_bands.fit: Often the best balance between housing quality, services, and commute
commute_bands.watchout: Traffic and gate choice can still change the real commute.

commute_bands.label: Lifestyle tradeoff
commute_bands.fit: May work for families wanting larger homes or specific schools
commute_bands.watchout: Can become painful for early reporting or frequent base errands.

gate_selection_logic: Start with the user's likely duty location.
Identify the most realistic primary gate.
Compare the commute during the user's actual report window.
Do not choose a neighborhood based only on mileage.
commute_intelligence.user_instruction: Before signing a lease or contract, test the commute from the exact property address to the expected gate during the same time window you would normally report to work.

bah_market_reality.bah_bluf: Travis housing decisions should compare BAH against rent, mortgage, utilities, debt, and commute. BAH is a decision filter, not a promise of affordability.
recommended_calculations: BAH with dependents vs estimated rent
BAH without dependents vs estimated rent
Total housing cost vs 30% income lane
Residual monthly income after expenses and housing
bah_market_reality.base_tab_message: Show BAH as a decision filter for Travis, not a promise of affordability. Push users into PCSUnited financial calculators when the housing gap looks tight.

family_readiness.family_bluf: Families PCSing to Travis should verify school zones, childcare options, medical access, commute reality, and housing availability before choosing a neighborhood.

school_guidance.school_zone_warning: Always verify school assignment by exact address before signing a lease or purchase contract.
school_guidance.nearby_school_district_note: Vacaville Unified and TUSD schools in Fairfield/Vacaville are highly rated.
school_guidance.school_liaison_note: Use official school liaison resources where available for military school transition support.

childcare_guidance.cdc_note: Child Development Center availability, waitlists, and eligibility should be verified directly with official base resources.
childcare_guidance.youth_program_note: Youth programs and school-age care can materially affect family housing decisions.

medical_guidance.primary_medical_anchor: Travis Air Force Base medical services
medical_guidance.medical_warning: Verify clinic availability, pharmacy procedures, and emergency care options directly with official medical sources.

family_readiness.spouse_employment_note: The broader Fairfield / Vacaville / Dixon metro may offer spouse employment options, but commute, childcare, and credentialing should be considered together.

before_arrival: Verify official gate and Visitor Control Center hours.
Confirm sponsor contact and unit reporting instructions.
Confirm lodging or temporary housing plan.
Review housing office guidance before signing any lease or purchase contract.
Compare BAH against rent, mortgage, utilities, and debt.
Shortlist neighborhoods by likely gate and commute band.
Verify school zones by exact property address.
first_week: Visit or contact the housing office if still deciding between on-base and off-base options.
Drive the commute from target neighborhoods during expected report time.
Tour properties before committing when possible.
first_30_days: Finalize housing only after comparing commute, BAH fit, school zones, and total monthly cost.
Save emergency, medical, gate, and housing office contact information.
Update PCSUnited profile and financial readiness dashboard with final housing numbers.

pcs_watchouts.title: Gate hours can change
pcs_watchouts.message: Use official Travis Air Force Base gate pages before travel or commute planning.

pcs_watchouts.title: Distance is not the same as commute
pcs_watchouts.message: Neighborhoods should be compared by realistic gate access and traffic timing.

pcs_watchouts.title: School zones are address-specific
pcs_watchouts.message: Never assume a school assignment from neighborhood name alone.

pcs_watchouts.title: BAH is not the full affordability picture
pcs_watchouts.message: Users must compare rent or mortgage against income, debt, expenses, utilities, and savings goals.

pcs_watchouts.title: Property taxes and insurance shape true payment
pcs_watchouts.message: In this market, taxes (~1.12%) and insurance assumptions must be included in all-in comparisons.

common_objections: Affordability gap between BAH and mortgage
recommended_agent_behavior: Always recommend school-zone verification by address.
Use payment math, not just listing price.
Avoid generic Fairfield / Vacaville / Dixon advice when the user is assigned to Travis.

base_profile.disclaimer: PCSUnited is not an official DoD source. This Base tab is intended for public-facing PCS orientation, housing research, and relocation planning. Users should verify gate hours, access requirements, medical services, housing availability, school zones, and official procedures directly with the installation or official service provider before travel, lease signing, or home purchase.

demographics.provider: US Census Bureau / Solano County EDC

base_profile.provider: PCSUnited planning profile using public-facing installation, housing, and market research structure

parser_notes: This Travis AFB file was upgraded to the Lackland-style PCSUnited schema with slug, name, state_code, profile, market_metrics, metrics, ownership_costs, costs, rental_metrics, summary_points, risks, opportunities, buyer_notes, seller_notes, investor_angles, and guidance arrays.
Fairfield / Vacaville / Dixon is preserved as the surrounding metro/submarket context; base-specific market data was retained.
property_tax_rate and insurance_rate are normalized to decimal form for deterministic math, with percent aliases preserved for display compatibility.

This file now includes base_profile for the PCSUnited Base tab, including gate orientation, visitor control center, major services, on-base housing, recommended nearby neighborhoods, commute intelligence, BAH market guidance, PCS watchouts, and source notes.
